steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Mark the given angle relationships on the figure first: equal angles, vertical angles, supplementary angles, parallel-line angles, and triangle sums of $180^\circ$. Most of these questions become a short angle chase once the givens are marked.

steps.title: Identify corresponding angles in the congruent triangles
steps.content: Since triangles ABC and DEF are congruent with A ↔ D and B ↔ E, the correspondence is complete: A ↔ D, B ↔ E, and C ↔ F. In triangle ABC, we know ∠A = 64° and ∠B = 90°.

steps.title: Use angle sum to find angle F
steps.content:
In triangle ABC, the sum of angles equals 180°:
$$64^{\circ} + 90^{\circ} + \angle C = 180^{\circ}$$
$$\angle C = 26^{\circ}$$
Since C corresponds to F, ∠F = 26°.
